title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Dark Circles
intro: Herbal teas for Dark circles are a natural remedy that refers to a type of tea made from herbs, flowers, or plants that are used to reduce the appearance of dark circles under the eyes. These teas can treat this ailment by improving circulation, reducing puffiness, and providing antioxidants that help to combat the underlying causes of dark circles. Examples of herbal teas that can help to treat dark circles include peppermint tea, which helps to improve circulation and reduce puffiness, chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the skin and reduce swelling, lavender tea, which has a calming effect that can help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, green tea, which is rich in antioxidants that can help to protect the skin from damage and promote collagen production, and rosehip tea, which is rich in vitamin C that can help to improve circulation and reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, other herbal teas such as hawthorn tea, which helps to improve circulation and reduce blood pressure, ginseng tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce puffiness and improve circulation, and licorice root tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the skin and reduce swelling, can also be effective in treating dark circles.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat dark circles, a common concern for many individuals. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes, thereby addressing the dark circles ailment. The bioactive constituents present in Ginkgo biloba teas, including flavonoids and terpenoids, play a crucial role in improving blood circulation and reducing oxidative stress, which contribute to the appearance of dark circles. By consuming Ginkgo biloba teas, individuals can experience the benefits of improved circulation, reduced inflammation, and a more radiant complexion, making it a popular natural remedy for treating dark circles.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and add them to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Let the tea cool down.
Drink 1 cup of the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce dark circles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help reduce the appearance of dark circles under the eyes, but its use may be associated with side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid mixing with other medications or herbal supplements, and discontinue use if any adverse reactions occur.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2 times a day.
Use 1 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba tea powder per cup.
Take Ginkgo biloba tea for 4-6 weeks to see noticeable results.
Combine with a balanced diet and regular exercise for best results.
Monitor your dark circles and adjust dosage if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, rich in antioxidants and essential oils, have been traditionally used to treat dark circles under the eyes. The anti-inflammatory and astringent properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce puffiness and discoloration, promoting a healthier and more radiant complexion. The bioactive constituents, including camphor, borneol, and rosmarinic acid, help to improve circulation, reduce swelling, and protect the skin from oxidative damage. By incorporating Rosmarinus officinalis teas into your skincare routine, you can enjoy the benefits of reduced dark circles, improved skin tone, and a more youthful appearance.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 teaspoon dried Rosmarinus officinalis, 1 cup boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis and place it in a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Rosmarinus officinalis in the tea infuser or strainer.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or strainer from the cup.
Strain the tea into a separate cup and let it cool before applying it to the affected area with a cotton pad.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "RSC Advances", Rosmarinus officinalis teas for dark circles may be effective due to its potent antioxidant and anti-aging activities, which help to inhibit the activities of tyrosinase, elastase, and collagenase enzymes that contribute to skin aging.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating dark circles, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals, especially if consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, monitor your body's response, and avoid combining it with other stimulants or medications that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a week before bedtime.
Continue for 4-6 weeks for noticeable improvement in dark circles.
Combine with a balanced diet and regular sleep for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat dark circles under the eyes due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Silybum marianum, including silymarin and flavonoids, help to reduce puffiness and darkening of the skin by inhibiting the formation of melanin and improving blood circulation. The herbal preparation's ability to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin also contributes to its effectiveness in treating dark circles. Regular consumption of Silybum marianum tea may help to improve the appearance of the under-eye area, reducing the appearance of dark circles and promoting a more radiant complexion.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Silybum marianum leaves, or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Silybum marianum leaves to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool down.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce dark circles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat dark circles, but its use may be associated with side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Silybum marianum teas to treat dark circles, it is essential to take precautions like monitoring your body's response and starting with a low dose to avoid adverse effects like nausea and dizziness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea daily in the morning.
Take 1 tablespoon of Silybum marianum extract 30 minutes before bed.
Use cold compress on dark circles for 5 minutes, 2 times a day.
Get 7-8 hours of sleep each night for better skin health.
Drink Silybum marianum tea for 2-3 months to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to treat dark circles due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids in Hypericum perforatum teas help to reduce puffiness and discoloration by improving blood circulation and protecting the skin from oxidative stress. Drinking Hypericum perforatum teas may help to alleviate dark circles by reducing the appearance of blood vessels and promoting collagen production, thereby giving the skin a brighter and more even tone. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat dark circles include reduced appearance of dark circles, improved skin texture, and a natural and non-invasive approach to addressing this common aesthetic concern.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Hypericum perforatum leaves. Use 1 tablespoon of fresh leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool down. You can store it in the fridge for up to 24 hours.
Drink 1/2 cup of the cooled tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce dark circles. You can also add a few ice cubes for a refreshing drink.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can cause mild gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, stomach upset, and diarrhea when consumed in excess to treat dark circles, particularly if taken for extended periods. Precautions include avoiding consumption of Hypericum perforatum teas during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as well as when taking medications for diabetes, blood thinners, and sedatives, to minimize interactions and side effects.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Hypericum perforatum tea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Let the tea cool down and refrigerate for 30 minutes to an hour.
Soak 2 cotton pads in the cooled tea and place them under your eyes.
Leave the pads on for 15-20 minutes, then discard and rinse your eyes with cold water.
Repeat this process 2-3 times a week for optimal results and 1-2 times a day for maintenance.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat dark circles due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing puffiness and promoting blood circulation, which in turn helps to fade the appearance of dark circles. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to combat the underlying causes of dark circles. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat dark circles include reduced puffiness, improved skin tone, and a more refreshed and rested appearance.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Place the dried flowers in the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool.
Drink 1 cup of the cooled tea twice a day to help reduce dark circles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat dark circles due to its potential to reduce puffiness and improve circulation, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headache, or stomach upset in some individuals. When using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat dark circles, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with small amounts and gradually increasing the dose, avoiding use if experiencing pregnancy or breastfeeding, and being cautious when combining with other medications or herbal preparations.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day.
Soak a cotton pad in the cooled tea and apply under eyes.
Leave the tea on for 10-15 minutes to reduce dark circles.
Use Lavandula angustifolia tea for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, derived from the dandelion plant, have been traditionally used to treat dark circles under the eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat dark circles by improving blood circulation, reducing puffiness, and eliminating toxins that cause discoloration. The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum officinale teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, play a crucial role in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to dark circles. Regular consumption of Taraxacum officinale teas may help to reduce the appearance of dark circles, promote healthy skin, and improve overall eye health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers, or 1/2 cup of dried Taraxacum officinale.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water in a pot.
Add the Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers to the boiling water, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Let the tea cool down, then apply it to the affected area with a cotton pad to help reduce dark circles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat dark circles, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching, and stomach upset, including nausea and diarrhea. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with a low dose, monitor their body's response, and avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, as this may exacerbate stomach upset.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 cup of Taraxacum officinale tea for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Soak a cotton pad in the cooled tea and apply under eyes.
Use 2-3 times a week for best results, in the morning or evening.
Continue for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable reduction in dark circles.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate dark circles due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid, in this herbal preparation may help to reduce puffiness and discoloration by promoting blood circulation and protecting the delicate skin around the eyes. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea may also help to reduce fatigue and improve overall eye health, which can contribute to a more radiant and rested appearance. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat dark circles include their potential to not only reduce the appearance of dark circles but also to promote overall well-being and reduce the risk of related health issues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Steep 1 cup of boiling water with the dried flowers for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers.
Let the tea cool down, then refrigerate it for up to 2 days.
Soak a cotton pad with the tea and apply it to the dark circles for 5-10 minutes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat dark circles, but it may cause some side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, avoid taking it in large doses, especially if you have sensitive skin or allergies, and be cautious when consuming it with other herbal preparations or medications, such as blood thinners.

Cool the tea and refrigerate it for at least 30 minutes.
Soak two cotton pads in the cooled tea.
Place the soaked pads under your eyes for 10-15 minutes daily.
Use 1 teaspoon of Echinacea purpurea extract in 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a week.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat dark circles under the eyes, thanks to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents present in Aloe barbadensis, such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E, help to reduce puffiness and discoloration. The anti-inflammatory properties of these teas help to constrict blood vessels and reduce the appearance of dark circles, while the antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and further exacerbate the condition. By promoting healthy circulation and reducing inflammation, Aloe barbadensis teas can help to improve the appearance of the under-eye area and provide relief from dark circles.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 2 tablespoons of Aloe barbadensis gel, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Cut the Aloe barbadensis leaf and extract the gel. Avoid the yellow sap as it may cause skin irritation.
Boil the water and let it cool for 5 minutes. This helps prevent the Aloe gel from becoming bitter.
Add the Aloe gel to the tea infuser or strainer, and steep it in the cooled water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool completely before storing it in the fridge for up to 3 days. Drink 1 cup of the tea before bedtime to help reduce dark circles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be used to treat dark circles, but it may cause some side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues, especially when consumed in excess or for extended periods. To use Aloe barbadensis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dosage, monitor your body's reaction, and avoid drinking it before bedtime or with other medications, as it may interfere with their efficacy or cause interactions.
preparation_usage: Wash your face with mild soap and water daily.
Soak a cotton pad with Aloe barbadensis tea.
Dab the soaked cotton pad under eyes gently.
Leave it for 10-15 minutes, 2-3 times a week.
Drink 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis tea daily for overall benefits.

Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea for taste. Stir well.
Let the tea cool down. Soak two cotton pads in the tea and place them under your eyes for 10-15 minutes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ruscus aculeatus teas can be used to treat dark circles, but it may cause side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including drinking the tea in moderation, avoiding consumption on an empty stomach, and being aware of potential allergic reactions to the plant's compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon teas, also known as cranberry tea, have been traditionally used to treat dark circles under the eyes due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The anthocyanins and flavonoids present in Vaccinium macrocarpon teas help to reduce puffiness and discoloration, thereby alleviating the appearance of dark circles. By promoting blood circulation and reducing inflammation, Vaccinium macrocarpon teas can help to minimize the visibility of dark circles, leaving the skin looking brighter and more radiant. The benefits of using Vaccinium macrocarpon teas to treat dark circles include reduced eye fatigue, improved skin tone, and a more even complexion.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Vaccinium macrocarpon berries, fresh or dried.
Combine 1 cup of berries with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the liquid to sweeten. Stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce dark circles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vaccinium macrocarpon teas can be used to potentially alleviate dark circles, but it may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. When using Vaccinium macrocarpon teas to treat this ailment, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with a low dose, drinking plenty of water, and avoiding consumption before bedtime to minimize the risk of side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat dark circles, a common condition characterized by discoloration and puffiness under the eyes. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis, including alkaloids such as quinine and quinidine, help to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating dark circles. The herbal preparation also exhibits antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and promote collagen production, further contributing to the reduction of dark circles. The benefits of Cinchona officinalis teas in treating dark circles include improved circulation, reduced puffiness, and a more even-toned complexion, making it a popular natural remedy for this cosmetic concern.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1/4 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Place the dried bark in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the Cinchona officinalis bark.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a week, ideally before bedtime, to help reduce dark circles under the eyes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat dark circles, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its high quinine content, which can also interact with certain medications and worsen underlying conditions. To minimize potential issues, individuals should start with small doses, monitor their body's response, and avoid taking the tea if they have conditions such as bleeding disorders or are pregnant, as well as avoiding it with certain medications like blood thinners and diabetes medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat dark circles due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing puffiness and promoting blood circulation, thus brightening the under-eye area. The bioactive constituents of Camellia sinensis teas, including catechins and theaflavins, help to combat oxidative stress and protect against damage caused by free radicals. The benefits of using Camellia sinensis teas to treat dark circles include improved skin health, reduced appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, and a more refreshed and revitalized appearance.
preparation_procedure: Measure 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of loose Camellia sinensis leaves (black, green, or oolong) for every 8 oz cup.
Steep the leaves for 3-5 minutes, depending on your tea preference.
Strain the tea leaves using a tea infuser or a fine-mesh sieve.
Let the tea cool down before applying it to the affected area.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea, wring it out, and apply it to the dark circles for 5-10 minutes.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study on polyphenols from medicinal plants, Camellia sinensis teas for dark circles may be beneficial due to its high phenolic content, including catechin, luteolin, and quercetin, which have antioxidant properties that may help reduce the appearance of dark circles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used as a potential treatment for dark circles, but its use may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its caffeine content and other compounds. To minimize potential side effects, it's recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase, avoid taking Camellia sinensis teas too close to bedtime, and be cautious when combining it with other stimulants or medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat dark circles, a common issue characterized by the appearance of dark shadows under the eyes. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce puffiness and discoloration associated with dark circles. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to their dark circle-treating effects. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been shown to provide benefits in alleviating dark circles, including improved skin health, reduced eye strain, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the root.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and mix well.
Drink the tea once a day, preferably before bedtime, for 2-3 weeks to see noticeable results.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat dark circles, but possible side effects may include increased heart rate, high blood pressure, and fluid retention due to its licorice root content. Precautions when using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas include limiting the duration of use and monitoring sodium intake, as excessive consumption can lead to potassium depletion and worsen the condition.
